title: Google Trends search-interest is contemporaneous (reverse-causal), not predictive, for BTC/ETH/XAU
why: Test Preis-2013-style search-interest -> forward-return predictability on BTC/ETH/XAU weekly, while guarding the Fear&Greed reverse-causality trap.
value_rationale: Clean negative closes an alt-data avenue; contemporaneous-only confirms the reverse-causal prior.
scope: FEASIBILITY GATE PASSED: pytrends weekly, 265 wks 2021-2026, cached cache/trends/google_trends_weekly_raw.csv. PHASE 2: predictive (1-wk lag) framing is NULL OOS - no (asset,signal) clears sign-consistent train+holdout IC with holdout hit>53% and positive Sharpe. CONTEMPORANEOUS control is the real signal: wow_change interest[t] vs return[t] IC train->holdout +0.08->+0.10 (BTC), +0.17->+0.13 (ETH), hit 0.56-0.64, Sharpe +0.7..+1.0 BOTH splits; the SAME signal lagged collapses (IC gap +0.18-0.19). Searches move WITH price same-week, carry no next-week info.
recommendation: Do NOT build a Google Trends predictive primary. Avenue closed as a directional next-week predictor.

history.reason: feat(spike): Google Trends -- null OOS, reverse-causal (searches move WITH price, no forward info); B0084 done
